title: Binary Tree Diameter
description:
The diameter of a binary tree is the length of the longest path between any two nodes (path length = number of nodes on the path).

The tree is given in level-order (BFS) with 'N' for null nodes.
inputSpec: A single line: level-order encoding (values and 'N'). Example: 1 2 3 N N 4 5
outputSpec: The diameter (number of nodes on the longest path).

botCodes.code:
vals=input().split()
n=len(vals)
# Build tree: index 0-based
left=[-1]*n;right=[-1]*n
for i in range(n):
    l,r=2*i+1,2*i+2
    if l<n and vals[l]!='N':left[i]=l
    if r<n and vals[r]!='N':right[i]=r

def diam(i):
    if i<0 or vals[i]=='N':return 0,0
    hl,dl=diam(left[i])
    hr,dr=diam(right[i])
    h=1+max(hl,hr)
    d=max(dl,dr,hl+hr+1)
    return h,d

h,d=diam(0)
print(d)
